forecast.name: Overnight
forecast.detailedForecast: A chance of rain. Mostly cloudy, with a low around 55. Southwest wind around 8 mph. Chance of precipitation is 40%. New rainfall amounts less than a tenth of an inch possible.

forecast.name: Tuesday
forecast.detailedForecast: Rain likely. Mostly cloudy. High near 64, with temperatures falling to around 61 in the afternoon. South southwest wind 7 to 10 mph. Chance of precipitation is 60%. New rainfall amounts less than a tenth of an inch possible.

weather_report:
In the nocturnal stillness of Downtown San Francisco, a gentle blanket of clouds wraps the city, harboring the promise of rain as the night deepens. The air carries the cool whisper of the sea, hinting at a forecast where chance and certainty entwine in a delicate balance. With a low around 55, comfort is found in layers, as the soft breeze from the southwest teases the remnants of warmth. 

As dawn approaches, the skies are likely to sustain their gray countenance, with rain softly cascading later in the day. Highs reaching around 64 will begin with a fleeting warmth before retreating into a cool embrace. The chance of precipitation—now resting at a gentle 60%—carries with it an invitation to indulge in cozy pursuits. Should you venture out, a light jacket accompanied by a trusty umbrella may provide comfort against the impending sprinkles. The day unfurls with quiet possibilities: perhaps a stroll through one of the city's serene parks or a visit to a cozy café, where the warmth of coffee and conversation can meld with the rhythmic sound of raindrops against the window. While the fog may linger alongside the marine layer at the edges, it offers an atmospheric charm, softening the edges of the towering skyline and embracing the unique beauty of this coastal enclave.
